TextWindow.description: Provides text-based input and output operations for console-style programs, enabling reading user input from the keyboard and writing formatted text output to the screen.

WriteLine.description: Writes a line of text to the text window followed by a newline character.

Write.description: Writes text to the text window without appending a newline.

Read.description: Reads a full line of text input from the user.

ReadNumber.description: Reads a numeric value entered by the user.

ForegroundColor.description: Gets or sets the foreground color of text displayed in the window.

BackgroundColor.description: Gets or sets the background color of the text window.

Clear.description: Clears all text content from the text window.

Pause.description: Pauses execution and waits for the user to press any key.

GraphicsWindow.description: Provides a graphical drawing surface for creating shapes, images, and animations with mouse and keyboard interaction support for building visual Small Basic programs.

DrawRectangle.description: Draws a rectangle outline at the specified coordinates with given dimensions.

FillRectangle.description: Draws a filled rectangle at the specified coordinates using the current brush color.

DrawEllipse.description: Draws an ellipse outline bounded by the specified rectangle.

DrawLine.description: Draws a line between two coordinate points on the graphics surface.

DrawText.description: Draws text at the specified position using the current font and brush color.

Show.description: Displays the graphics window on screen.

Width.description: Gets or sets the width of the graphics window in pixels.

Height.description: Gets or sets the height of the graphics window in pixels.

BrushColor.description: Gets or sets the brush color used for filling shapes and drawing text.

PenColor.description: Gets or sets the pen color used for drawing shape outlines and lines.

MouseDown.description: Event raised when a mouse button is pressed on the graphics window.

KeyDown.description: Event raised when a key is pressed while the graphics window has focus.

Math.description: Provides standard mathematical functions and constants including trigonometry, rounding, random number generation, and common numeric operations for calculations.

Abs.description: Returns the absolute value of a specified number.

Ceiling.description: Returns the smallest integer greater than or equal to the specified number.

Floor.description: Returns the largest integer less than or equal to the specified number.

GetRandomNumber.description: Returns a random integer between 1 and the specified maximum value.

Max.description: Returns the larger of two specified numbers.

Min.description: Returns the smaller of two specified numbers.

SquareRoot.description: Returns the square root of a specified number.

Pi.description: Returns the value of the mathematical constant pi (approximately 3.14159).

Sin.description: Returns the sine of the specified angle in radians.

Cos.description: Returns the cosine of the specified angle in radians.

Clock.description: Provides access to system clock values including current date components, time components, and elapsed milliseconds for timing and date-based program logic.

Day.description: Gets the current day of the month.

ElapsedMilliseconds.description: Gets the number of milliseconds elapsed since midnight.

Timer.description: Provides a repeating timer mechanism that raises tick events at a configurable interval, useful for animations, periodic updates, and time-based game logic.

Interval.description: Gets or sets the interval between timer tick events in milliseconds.

Tick.description: Event raised each time the timer interval elapses.

Pause.description: Pauses the timer to stop tick events from being raised.

Controls.description: Provides user interface controls such as buttons and text boxes that can be placed on the graphics window for building interactive programs with form-like input.

AddButton.description: Creates a button with specified caption at the given position and returns its identifier.

AddTextBox.description: Creates a text input box at the given position and returns its identifier.

AddMultiLineTextBox.description: Creates a multi-line text input box at the given position and returns its identifier.

GetTextBoxText.description: Returns the current text content of the specified text box control.

SetTextBoxText.description: Sets the text content of the specified text box control.

ButtonClicked.description: Event raised when any button control is clicked by the user.

LastClickedButton.description: Returns the name of the button that was most recently clicked.

Shapes.description: Provides named shape objects on the graphics window that can be individually moved, rotated, animated, and hidden, enabling sprite-based graphics and simple game development.

AddRectangle.description: Adds a rectangle shape and returns its identifier for later manipulation.

AddEllipse.description: Adds an ellipse shape and returns its identifier for later manipulation.

AddTriangle.signature: Shapes.AddTriangle(x1, y1, x2, y2, x3, y3) -> String
AddTriangle.description: Adds a triangle shape defined by three vertices and returns its identifier.

AddImage.description: Adds an image as a shape and returns its identifier for later manipulation.

Move.description: Moves the specified shape to a new position instantly.

Animate.description: Smoothly animates the shape to a new position over the specified duration in milliseconds.

Rotate.description: Rotates the shape by the specified angle in degrees.

Remove.description: Removes the specified shape from the graphics window.
